member.name: Rosie Duffield
member.synopsis: Rosie Duffield is the Independent MP for Canterbury , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Access to Work Programme: Medical Examinations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if he will set out how his Department defines the distinction between enabling support and replacement support for the purposes of Access to Work assessments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Enabling support and replacement support may be provided by a Support Worker job aide when a customer is unable to do part or all of their job because of their disability, illness or health condition. If a customer is performing the job role, making decis...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will set out which stakeholders were consulted in the decision-making process of changes to the Police Pension Scheme 1987.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There have been no recent changes to the rules which govern the 1987 police pension scheme. The 1987 scheme is now closed for further accrual of pension benefits and there are no plans to make changes to it.There may be changes from time to time to the fa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of changes to the Police Pension Scheme 1987 on serving and retired scheme members.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There have been no recent changes to the rules which govern the 1987 police pension scheme. The 1987 scheme is now closed for further accrual of pension benefits and there are no plans to make changes to it.There may be changes from time to time to the fa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Access to Work Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what guidance his Department has provided to Access to Work case managers on assessing applications; and when that guidance was last updated.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Access to Work case managers assess applications in line with the Department’s published Access to Work: staff guide - GOV.UK, available on GOV.UK. This guidance provides the framework for assessing applications and determining entitlement to Access to Wo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to publish criteria and guidance, so schools understand how funding decisions are made, what evidence is required, who makes those decisions, and how consistency is ensured.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department publishes a range of guidance each year to support schools and local authorities in understanding how funding is allocated, available at the links below.National funding formula policy papers: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/nati...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that local authorities operate a clear system for accessing high needs top up funding, in the context of her Department's High Needs operational guidance.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local authorities have flexibility to determine how to allocate high needs funding, including top up funding, in line with their statutory duties under the Children and Families Act 2014. In doing so, they must take account of the costs that would reasona...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Landfill: Environmental Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to assess whether sites like the Shelford landfill have followed environmental permit rules.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Waste management sites are regulated through environmental permits to ensure best practice and minimise environmental impact. Permits set conditions requiring operators to accept only appropriate waste, prevent pollution, and avoid nuisances like odours a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, which stakeholders were consulted in the decision-making process of changes to the Police Pension Scheme 1987.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Changes to the Police Pension Scheme 1987 are a matter for the Home Secretary as the responsible authority for the scheme.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of changes to the Police Pension Scheme 1987 on serving and retired scheme members.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Changes to the Police Pension Scheme 1987 are a matter for the Home Secretary as the responsible authority for the scheme.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential implications for her policies of the recent notification of changes to the Police Pension Scheme 1987, including the rationale for the proposed amendments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Changes to the Police Pension Scheme 1987 are a matter for the Home Secretary as the responsible authority for the scheme.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Canterbury
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what recent assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of new housing developments on water infrastructure in Canterbury.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Water companies, including South East Water, are under a statutory duty to produce Water Resources Management Plans and Drainage and Wastewater Management Plans. In response to Canterbury’s Local Plan consultation, the Environment Agency has recommended c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Meningitis: Health Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department plans to fund sustained public awareness activity on meningitis, including awareness of signs and symptoms, available vaccinations and when to seek urgent medical help.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Raising awareness about the signs and symptoms of meningitis and septicaemia, as well as the benefits of the MenACWY vaccine, remains a Department funding priority.The Department, UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) and NHS England have been working with na...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: Raising awareness in parents, teenagers, and young adults about the signs and symptoms of meningitis and septicaemia, as well as the benefits of the MenACWY vaccine, remains a priority.The Department, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA), and NHS England...

writtenQuestions.heading: Meningitis: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department plans to undertake an (a) equality impact assessment and (b) health inequalities assessment of access to meningococcal group B vaccination.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In developing vaccination policy, the Department has due regard to its duties under the Public Sector Equality Duty. Consideration of equality and health inequalities impacts are undertaken as part of the development, implementation, and review of vaccina...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
